(a). , substituted “In general” for “Corporations” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of this subtitle, in the case of a corporation, the term ‘taxable income’ means gross income minus the deductions allowed by this chapter.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 102(a)Subsec. (b). , substituted “Individuals who do not itemize their deductions” for “Individuals” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of this subtitle, in the case of an individual, the term ‘taxable income’ means adjusted gross income—Pub. L. 99–514, § 102(a)“(1) reduced by the sum of—“(A) the excess itemized deductions,“(B) the deductions for personal exemptions provided by section 151, and“(C) the direct charitable deduction, and“(2) increased (in the case of an individual for whom an unused zero bracket amount computation is provided by subsection (e)) by the unused zero bracket amount (if any).”Subsec. (c). , substituted “Standard deduction” for “Excess itemized deductions” in heading and amended text generally. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), for purposes of this subtitle, the term “taxable income” means gross income minus the deductions allowed by this chapter (other than the standard deduction).

children.heading: Individuals who do not itemize their deductions
children.label: b Individuals who do not itemize their deductions

children.text: the standard deduction,
the deduction for personal exemptions provided in section 151,
any deduction provided in section 199A,
the deduction provided in section 170(p),
the deduction provided in section 224,
the deduction provided in section 225 and1So in original. Probably should be preceded by a comma.1
so much of the deduction allowed by section 163(a) as is attributable to the exception under section 163(h)(4)(A).

children.label: A
children.text: the basic standard deduction, and

children.label: B
children.text: the additional standard deduction.

children.label: i
children.text: a joint return, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a surviving spouse (as defined in section 2(a)),

children.label: B
children.text: $4,400 in the case of a head of household (as defined in section 2(b)), or

children.label: C
children.text: $3,000 in any other case.

children.heading: Additional standard deduction for aged and blind
children.label: 3 Additional standard deduction for aged and blind
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the additional standard deduction is the sum of each additional amount to which the taxpayer is entitled under subsection (f).

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: i
children.text: “calendar year 1987” in the case of the dollar amounts contained in paragraph (2)(B), (2)(C), or (5)(A) or subsection (f), and

children.label: ii
children.text: “calendar year 1997” in the case of the dollar amount contained in paragraph (5)(B).

children.heading: Limitation on basic standard deduction in the case of certain dependents
children.label: 5 Limitation on basic standard deduction in the case of certain dependents
B
children.text: the sum of $250 and such individual’s earned income.

children.heading: Certain individuals, etc., not eligible for standard deduction
children.label: 6 Certain individuals, etc., not eligible for standard deduction
A
children.text: a married individual filing a separate return where either spouse itemizes deductions,

children.label: B
children.text: a nonresident alien individual,

children.label: C
children.text: an individual making a return under section 443(a)(1) for a period of less than 12 months on account of a change in his annual accounting period, or

children.label: D
children.text: an estate or trust, common trust fund, or partnership,

children.heading: Special rules for taxable years beginning after 2017
children.label: 7 Special rules for taxable years beginning after 2017
i
children.text: by substituting “$23,625” for “$4,400” in subparagraph (B), and

children.label: ii
children.text: by substituting “$15,750” for “$3,000” in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Paragraph (4) shall not apply to the dollar amounts contained in paragraphs (2)(B) and (2)(C).

children.label: I
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: II
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “2024” for “2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.
the deductions allowable in arriving at adjusted gross income, and
any deduction referred to in any paragraph of subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Unless an individual makes an election under this subsection for the taxable year, no itemized deduction shall be allowed for the taxable year. For purposes of this subtitle, the determination of whether a deduction is allowable under this chapter shall be made without regard to the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Time and manner of election
children.label: 2 Time and manner of election
children.text: Any election under this subsection shall be made on the taxpayer’s return, and the Secretary shall prescribe the manner of signifying such election on the return.

children.label: A
children.text: the spouse makes a change of election with respect to itemized deductions, for the taxable year covered in such separate return, consistent with the change of treatment sought by the taxpayer, and

children.label: B
children.text: the taxpayer and his spouse consent in writing to the assessment (within such period as may be agreed on with the Secretary) of any deficiency, to the extent attributable to such change of election, even though at the time of the filing of such consent the assessment of such deficiency would otherwise be prevented by the operation of any law or rule of law.

children.heading: Aged or blind additional amounts
children.label: f Aged or blind additional amounts
A
children.text: for himself if he has attained age 65 before the close of his taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: for the spouse of the taxpayer if the spouse has attained age 65 before the close of the taxable year and an additional exemption is allowable to the taxpayer for such spouse under section 151(b).

children.label: A
children.text: for himself if he is blind at the close of the taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: for the spouse of the taxpayer if the spouse is blind as of the close of the taxable year and an additional exemption is allowable to the taxpayer for such spouse under section 151(b).

children.heading: Higher amount for certain unmarried individuals
children.label: 3 Higher amount for certain unmarried individuals
children.text: In the case of an individual who is not married and is not a surviving spouse, paragraphs (1) and (2) shall be applied by substituting “$750” for “$600”.

children.heading: Blindness defined
children.label: 4 Blindness defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, an individual is blind only if his central visual acuity does not exceed 20/200 in the better eye with correcting lenses, or if his visual acuity is greater than 20/200 but is accompanied by a limitation in the fields of vision such that the widest diameter of the visual field subtends an angle no greater than 20 degrees.

children.heading: Marital status
children.label: g Marital status
children.text: For purposes of this section, marital status shall be determined under section 7703.
